insight: shared package with tsconfig outDir=dist still emits JS/d.ts into src/ when rootDir=src. Always check for stale compiled files in src/ after build, and add *.js/*.d.ts to .gitignore for TS source dirs.

Vite/Rollup cannot resolve TypeScript enums from CJS-compiled shared packages. The shared package.json needs proper exports field with import/default conditions, and vite.config.ts may need commonjsOptions.include for enum resolution.

For monorepo with independent packages (server vs web), launch background agents in parallel. Each agent gets its own context window and can work independently without merge conflicts. Wait for both to complete, then verify builds sequentially.
Build order must be: shared → server → web. Shared must finish before server (TypeScript path resolution) and web (Vite module resolution). Use pnpm --filter to build in sequence.
